arts.ttl: Revealed: Why Arsenal youngster was booked BEFORE making debut v City!
arts.sum: Myles Lewis-Skelly received a yellow card before making his Premier League debut against Manchester City on Sunday - but what was the Arsenal youngster booked for?

arts.ttl: ‘They always mention’: Fabrizio Romano shares what he keeps being told about Martin Odegaard’s injury
arts.sum: Martin Odegaard is out injured at the moment, and he’s already missed two of Arsenal’s most important games of the season. The Arsenal captain has...

arts.ttl: Arteta rejects talk of Arsenal dark arts and insists team sheet will bear him out
arts.sum: ‘I always prefer the facts to supposing things’ Manager may start 16-year-old goalkeeper against Bolton Mikel Arteta has rejected the accusation from Manchester City that Arsenal employed the dark arts in Sunday’s 2-2 draw at the Eithad Stadium, saying the truth about his players’ condition would be revealed by his team sheet for Wednesday’s Carabao Cup tie at home to Bolton. City were furious at how a succession of Arsenal players went to ground and called for treatment during the second half as they tried to preserve a 2-1 lead with 10 menafter the dismissal of Leandro Trossard in first-half stoppage-time. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Arteta responds to 'dark arts' taunts by Man City as injuries mount for Arsenal
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta bristled at suggestions his team used “dark arts” in the feisty English Premier League match at Manchester City, saying he preferred to look at “facts” rather than opinions. This development came after Arsenal players were accused of play-acting and feigning injuries by some of their Man City counterparts during the 2-2 draw on Sunday at Etihad Stadium.

arts.ttl: Report: Real Madrid could offer £40m player to Arsenal in a swap deal for William Saliba
arts.sum: William Saliba is a very special player, and Arsenal are very lucky to have him. The Frenchman is undoubtedly one of the best in the...

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta suggests Arsenal player suffered 'serious injury' in Man City draw
arts.sum: Arsenal's understandably defensive performance was criticised by City players, who bemoaned of the visitors' 'dark arts' and time-wasting tactics used to try and run the clock down. Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta has claimed at least one of his players came away from Sunday's 2-2 draw at Manchester City with a'serious injury'.

arts.ttl: Latest Arsenal injury as seven miss Bolton after fresh triple worry emerges for Mikel Arteta
arts.sum: "Let’s see who is available tomorrow and then we can talk about dark arts," he told reporters. Alongside giving a detailed update on Odegaard, Arteta was then specifically asked about Raya and his answer unsurprisingly neither confirmed nor denied whether he would be available.

arts.ttl: Arsenal revealed as Premier League's biggest time-wasters after Man City clash
arts.sum: Arsenal's tactics against Manchester City in Sunday's dramatic draw have come under intense scrutiny, with a series of Pep Guardiola's players hitting out at the Gunners

arts.ttl: Why Arsenal new 'dream' signing is banned vs Bolton as Mikel Arteta forced to make tough call
arts.sum: Upon joining Arsenal, Neto described playing for the club as a dream. Sterling, however, who was also signed on loan from a Carabao Cup participating club in the summer, will be eligible to feature in the competition for Arsenal, having not featured in this season's tournament for Chelsea.

arts.ttl: Better than Merino: Romano confirms Arsenal interest in wonderkid
arts.sum: Several top clubs are keeping tabs on Eintracht Frankfurt midfielder Hugo Larsson, according to Fabrizio Romano. Larsson impressed in his first season in Germany and earned himself links to the likes of Arsenal and Liverpool .
